findings.meaning: Two or more FASTA records share the same identifier.
findings.why_it_matters: Duplicate IDs can break indexing, annotation, mapping, and workflow joins.

suggested_actions.reason: Downstream tools often assume FASTA identifiers are unique.

recommended_next_tools.role: inspect and rename duplicate records

findings.why_it_matters: Many downstream tools and submission validators index records by the first header token; collisions can affect submission readiness.

suggested_actions.reason: Tools that index by first token can retrieve or annotate the wrong record when first-token IDs collide.

recommended_next_tools.role: inspect and rename records with colliding first-token identifiers
validate repository-specific rules after FASTA-level identifier collisions are fixed

findings.description: One or more FASTA identifiers contain characters that may be unsafe for downstream tools.
findings.meaning: One or more FASTA identifiers contain characters that may be unsafe for downstream tools.
findings.why_it_matters: Some indexing, database, submission, and workflow tools assume simple portable record identifiers; unsafe characters can affect submission readiness.

suggested_actions.reason: Portable headers reduce surprises in indexes, database builders, submission validators, and tabular joins.

recommended_next_tools.role: normalize FASTA identifiers before indexing or submission
validate repository-specific rules after FASTA-level identifier issues are fixed

findings.description: One or more FASTA headers exceed the compatibility length limit.
findings.meaning: One or more FASTA headers exceed the compatibility length limit.
findings.why_it_matters: Long headers can be truncated or rejected by some database, indexing, and submission workflows, so they can affect submission readiness.

suggested_actions.reason: Portable headers reduce surprises in indexes, database builders, submission validators, and tabular joins.

recommended_next_tools.role: shorten headers or move descriptive metadata into sidecar tables
validate repository-specific header limits after FASTA-level issues are fixed

findings.description: One or more FASTA headers contain reserved compatibility characters.
findings.meaning: One or more FASTA headers contain reserved compatibility characters.
findings.why_it_matters: Reserved header characters can confuse parsers, database builders, or submission validators and can affect submission readiness.

suggested_actions.reason: Portable headers reduce surprises in indexes, database builders, submission validators, and tabular joins.

recommended_next_tools.role: normalize headers before database construction or submission
validate repository-specific header character rules after FASTA-level issues are fixed

findings.meaning: One or more sequences contain symbols outside the nucleotide/IUPAC alphabet accepted by the assembly profile.
findings.why_it_matters: Invalid characters can make parsers, aligners, or annotation tools fail or misinterpret records.

suggested_actions.reason: Invalid sequence symbols should be corrected or intentionally recoded before downstream analysis.

recommended_next_tools.role: inspect affected records

findings.meaning: Global or per-sequence N content exceeds the assembly profile threshold.
findings.why_it_matters: High N content can reduce mapping confidence, fragment annotation or polishing steps, and trigger submission-oriented ambiguity follow-up.

suggested_actions.reason: High ambiguity may indicate unresolved assembly regions or masking problems.
Assembly-level evaluation can show whether ambiguity affects broader assembly quality.

recommended_next_tools.role: assembly-level evaluation after FASTA validity issues are handled
biological completeness after structural preflight checks
database-backed contamination or adaptor screening when submission-oriented ambiguity signals need follow-up

findings.meaning: One or more contigs are shorter than the assembly profile minimum length.
findings.why_it_matters: Very short contigs often add noise to assembly statistics and downstream annotation.

suggested_actions.reason: Short records may be noise, but should be reviewed before automatic removal.

recommended_next_tools.role: filter or inspect short records

cannot_conclude: whether a tiny contig is biologically real

findings.meaning: The longest contiguous N gap run exceeds the assembly profile limit.
findings.why_it_matters: Long gap runs can indicate unresolved assembly regions, disrupt mapping or annotation, and trigger submission-oriented gap follow-up.

suggested_actions.reason: Long gaps may require gap closing, masking review, or scaffold-level inspection.

recommended_next_tools.role: assembly-level gap and scaffold evaluation
database-backed contamination or adaptor screening when submission-oriented gap signals need follow-up

findings.description: One or more records have leading or trailing N bases.
findings.meaning: One or more records have leading or trailing N bases.
findings.why_it_matters: Terminal Ns can trigger submission warnings and may indicate records that need trimming or scaffold-boundary review.

suggested_actions.target: records with leading or trailing N bases
suggested_actions.reason: Terminal Ns can trigger submission warnings and may indicate records that need trimming or scaffold-boundary review.

recommended_next_tools.role: inspect records with leading or trailing N bases
validate repository-specific rules after terminal N issues are reviewed or fixed

findings.description: One or more records contain repeated 100 bp N gap patterns.
findings.meaning: One or more records contain repeated 100 bp N gap patterns.
findings.why_it_matters: Repeated gap patterns can be legitimate scaffold placeholders, but they are often worth checking before submission or annotation.

suggested_actions.target: records with repeated 100 bp N gap patterns
suggested_actions.reason: Repeated placeholder-like gaps should be confirmed before submission or annotation workflows.

recommended_next_tools.role: evaluate scaffold gap patterns after FASTA preflight
validate repository-specific gap pattern rules after FASTA-level issues are reviewed or fixed

findings.description: Assembly ungapped total length falls outside the configured expected-size range.
findings.meaning: Assembly ungapped total length falls outside the configured expected-size range.
findings.why_it_matters: Unexpected assembly size can indicate missing sequence, extra sequence, contamination, or incorrect expected-size metadata.

suggested_actions.reason: Unexpected assembly size can indicate missing sequence, extra sequence, contamination, or incorrect expected-size metadata.

recommended_next_tools.role: compare assembly size against submission expectations
run deeper assembly QC after checking the expected-size metadata

findings.meaning: Two or more FASTA records contain identical sequence strings.
findings.why_it_matters: Duplicate sequences can inflate assembly metrics and confuse record-level comparisons.

suggested_actions.reason: Repeated sequence records may be expected in some contexts but should be explicit.

recommended_next_tools.role: deduplicate or inspect repeated sequences

findings.meaning: One or more records have GC composition far from the assembly background.
findings.why_it_matters: GC outliers can reflect contamination, cobionts, plasmids, artifacts, or genuine biological variation and need context before interpretation.

suggested_actions.reason: Records with GC composition far from the assembly background should be inspected with coverage and taxonomy context.
K-mer or taxonomy comparison can help distinguish artifacts from plausible biological variation.

recommended_next_tools.role: inspect composition, coverage, and taxonomy context for affected records
compare k-mer similarity when the pattern is strong

findings.meaning: One or more records have lengths outside the assembly length distribution.
findings.why_it_matters: Length outliers can be expected in assemblies, but extreme records deserve inspection before downstream filtering or annotation.

suggested_actions.reason: Extreme record lengths should be reviewed before automatic filtering or annotation.

recommended_next_tools.role: inspect sequence lengths and affected records

cannot_conclude: whether an extreme length is biologically expected

findings.meaning: One or more records combine a composition anomaly with another independent FASTA-level signal.
findings.why_it_matters: Multiple independent FASTA-level signals make a record more likely to need manual review before expensive downstream QC.

suggested_actions.reason: Records with multiple FASTA-level anomaly signals should be prioritized for composition and coverage review.

recommended_next_tools.role: prioritize composition and coverage review for records with multiple signals

findings.meaning: One or more samples have total assembly length that is unusual relative to the compare cohort.
findings.why_it_matters: A sample-level length outlier can indicate an input mix-up, incomplete assembly, extra sequence, or a cohort that should be split before downstream QC.

suggested_actions.reason: Samples with total length far from the cohort should be reviewed before batch downstream QC.

recommended_next_tools.role: inspect sequence counts and total lengths for affected samples
run assembly-level QC after FASTA preflight issues are understood

cannot_conclude: whether the cohort should share the same expected assembly size

findings.meaning: One or more samples have GC composition that is unusual relative to the compare cohort.
findings.why_it_matters: A sample-level GC outlier can indicate a cohort mismatch, sample swap, contamination signal, or real biological variation that needs context before interpretation.

suggested_actions.reason: Samples with GC composition far from the cohort should be reviewed before interpreting batch results.

recommended_next_tools.role: inspect composition and coverage context for affected samples after FASTA preflight
run assembly-level QC after FASTA preflight issues are understood

findings.meaning: The input file could not be parsed as structurally valid FASTA.
findings.why_it_matters: Structurally invalid FASTA cannot be parsed reliably by downstream tools.

suggested_actions.reason: Every record needs a valid header and sequence data before downstream tools can consume it safely.

recommended_next_tools.tool: text editor or parser-aware cleanup script
recommended_next_tools.role: repair malformed FASTA before rerunning FastaGuard
